Which kind of algorithm is Ron Rivest not famous for creating?
What is the name of the infamous software bug that caused a rocket to explode just 37 seconds after launch in 1996?
questions.explanation: The Ariane 5 rocket explosion was caused by a software error related to the conversion of a 64-bit floating point number to a 16-bit integer, which led to an overflow. This incident highlights the critical importance of software reliability in aerospace engineering.

questions.question: Which significant event in internet history, often referred to as the 'dot-com bubble', reached its peak in March 2000 before a dramatic fall?
questions.explanation: The dot-com crash, also known as the dot-com bubble, was a period of excessive speculation in internet-based companies during the late 1990s. This bubble peaked in March 2000 when many of these internet companies saw their stock prices skyrocket, only to crash dramatically in subsequent months, leading to significant financial losses and marking a pivotal moment in internet history.
